questions.question: What is the term for a problem that can be solved in a reasonable amount of time by a known algorithm?
questions.explanation: A problem that can be solved in a reasonable amount of time by a known algorithm.

questions.question: Which of the following problems is NP-Complete?
options: Searching for a specific word in a text
Finding the shortest path in a graph
questions.explanation: A problem that is NP-Complete is one that is in NP and every problem in NP can be reduced to it.

questions.question: What is the term for the set of all problems that can be solved in a reasonable amount of time by a known algorithm?
questions.explanation: The set of all problems that can be solved in a reasonable amount of time by a known algorithm.

questions.question: Which of the following problems is in NP but not NP-Complete?
options: Searching for a specific word in a text
Finding the shortest path in a graph
questions.explanation: A problem that is in NP but not NP-Complete is one that can be verified in polynomial time but is not NP-Complete.

questions.question: What is the term for the study of the limitations of algorithmic power?
questions.explanation: The study of the limitations of algorithmic power is known as the Limitations of Algorithmic Power.
